mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS held near the zero lower bound (0.12% as of Nov 2009), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns regardless of short-term news flow. The effect is always-on as long as the policy rate stays near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% Nov2009 near zero lower bound
regime_hint: any regime with FEDFUNDS near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during zero-bound periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE following a sustained multi-month rise (e.g., the Nov 2009 tick from 10.0% to 9.9%) signals an inflection point in labor market deterioration. This positive labor surprise triggers a short-horizon equity rally as investors price in reduced recession risk within the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases month-over-month after a preceding 3-month period of increases
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised down from 10.0 to 9.9 in Nov2009 vintage
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE decline show 5-day forward SPX positive-return rate not significantly above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (chi-square p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous recovery in INDPRO (industrial production) and RSAFS (retail sales) confirms that manufacturing rebound is demand-driven rather than pure inventory restocking. This joint confirmation signals a durable economic recovery that sustains S&P 500 gains over a quarterly (60-day) horizon; production recovery alone without retail confirmation is a weaker, potentially transient signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 2% concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and RSAFS both showing recovery trends by late 2009
failure_condition: When both proxies jointly trigger, 60-day forward SPX positive-return rate falls below the unconditional base rate, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC versus either single-series model is not statistically distinguishable (bootstrap p > 0.10) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In an early post-recession recovery regime with policy rates at the zero bound, stabilization of HOUST near multi-decade lows combined with a return to positive GDPC1 growth signals that the housing-sensitive demand channel is bottoming, historically preceding sustained equity gains over 20 trading days. This mechanism is regime-specific: in mature expansions housing stabilization carries little forward-return signal.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529-534k units, near historic lows, Oct2009
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with zero-bound monetary policy
failure_condition: During the identified early-recovery regime, 20-day forward SPX returns conditioned on HOUST stabilization plus positive GDPC1 growth do not exceed the unconditional base rate by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or the signal shows equal or opposite sign in mid/late-cycle regimes without adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the pace of PAYEMS job losses (3-month rolling average decline narrowing below 50,000 per month) signals imminent labor market stabilization, which historically precedes sustained equity market gains over a quarterly horizon as forward earnings expectations recover ahead of confirmed employment growth.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS monthly decline narrows to less than 50,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS decline slowing, Nov2009 vintage 130996k vs revised 130057k
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign when tested on the fully revised PAYEMS series versus the real-time vintage.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero FEDFUNDS combined with subdued CPIAUCSL inflation produces deeply negative real short-term interest rates, incentivizing rapid rotation into risk assets over a short 5-day horizon. Neither series alone is sufficient: a low nominal rate with rising inflation would not produce the same negative-real-rate incentive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 0.12% and CPIAUCSL 216.5 Oct2009 implying low inflation
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX positive-return rate during joint trigger periods is statistically indistinguishable from periods when only one condition holds (p > 0.10), invalidating the claimed interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects resilient consumer spending, a key driver of GDP, and is priced into equities with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon irrespective of specific trigger thresholds.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS recovering to 352073 million Nov2009 vintage from lower 2009 lows
regime_hint: any regime with positive RSAFS trend
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month trend and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS trend periods show no significant excess return versus baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of INDPRO year-over-year growth from negative to positive marks the technical end of a manufacturing recession, historically triggering a sustained equity rally over the following quarter as production expansion signals broader economic recovery beyond consumer spending alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth turns positive after being negative for at least 6 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO showing recovery trajectory by Nov2009 after deep 2008-2009 contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX positive-return rate following the INDPRO YoY sign-crossing trigger is not significantly above the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05), or the crossing signal produces negative returns in more than 40% of historical trigger instances.
